categories.title: SILVER SCREEN QUOTES
categories.theme: Famous lines spoken in Hollywood films, clued by the film they come from

clues.clue: Dory's three-word motivational mantra keeps the adventure going in this 2003 Pixar classic.
Chief Brody utters this classic line after his first close-up look at the great white shark in this 1975 thriller.
clues.answer: You're gonna need a bigger boat.

clues.clue: Harrison Ford plays the whip-cracking archaeologist who first appeared on screen in 1981's Raiders of the Lost Ark.
King Leonidas bellows this defiant battle cry to the Persian messenger in the 2006 film based on the Battle of Thermopylae.
F. Scott Fitzgerald's narrator opens his famous novel with these words, later adapted to film — and this actress appeared in both that film and American Horror Story.

categories.title: EUROPEAN CAPITALS & RIVERS
categories.theme: European capital cities and the rivers or geographic features associated with them

clues.clue: This European capital, home to the Prado museum and Royal Palace, sits on the Manzanares River.
Transylvania, the famed region associated with Dracula lore, lies within this modern European country.
This Caribbean twin-island nation's capital, Port of Spain, sits on the Gulf of Paria.
Norway adopted its constitution in this year, the same year Napoleon was exiled to Elba.
The Hindu Kush mountain range, stretching roughly 800 miles, runs primarily through this Central Asian country.

categories.title: KITCHEN & COCKTAIL
categories.theme: Food and drink facts covering dishes, ingredients, and iconic cocktails

clues.clue: Iceberg, Boston, and Bibb are all varieties of this leafy green used in salads.
This dark, dense German bread is made from rye and is a staple of Northern European cuisine.
Borscht, the vibrant beet-based soup, is most closely associated with this country's cuisine.
A kipper is a smoked, split, and cured version of this common North Atlantic fish.
This gin-based cocktail, featuring cherry liqueur and pineapple juice, was invented at the Raffles Hotel in Singapore.

categories.title: GREAT MINDS
categories.theme: Famous philosophers, authors, and artists and their landmark works or quotes

clues.clue: This English author completed his beloved Middle-earth saga with 'The Return of the King.'
Victor Hugo penned this novel set in medieval Paris, whose title character is the bell-ringer Quasimodo.
This Dutch Golden Age master painted the monumental group portrait known as 'Night Watch.'
Rodin's sculpture 'The Kiss' immortalizes this pair of ill-fated medieval lovers from Dante's Inferno.
This Enlightenment philosopher declared 'Man is born free, but is everywhere in chains' in his 1762 work 'The Social Contract.'

categories.title: SCIENCE CLASS
categories.theme: Science facts spanning physics, biology, astronomy, and natural phenomena

clues.clue: Forked, sheet, and ball are three distinct types of this dramatic atmospheric electrical discharge.
The SI unit for electric current, named after a French physicist, is this.
The colorful light displays seen near Earth's poles, caused by charged solar particles colliding with the atmosphere, share this name.
This large spiral galaxy, the closest to our own Milky Way, is visible to the naked eye on clear nights.
The Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish is remarkable for being considered potentially biologically immortal, capable of reverting to its juvenile state — it is commonly called this.

categories.title: WILD KINGDOM
categories.theme: Collective nouns and vocabulary about animals

clues.clue: This massive reptile kills its prey by coiling around it and squeezing — a method called constriction.
Snakes and lizards flick their tongues primarily to perform this function.
The Greek words for 'river' and 'horse' combine to give us the common name of this large African mammal.
A band of primates swinging through the jungle might collectively be called this — the same word used for a military unit.
Despite its fierce reputation, a group of bears shares its collective noun with this slow-moving insect-eating mammal.

categories.title: FOOD FOR THOUGHT

clues.clue: This Italian almond liqueur takes its name from the Italian word for 'bitter'.
This wheel-shaped pasta's name literally means 'little wheels' in Italian.
Known as the 'gold of the Incas,' this high-protein grain is related to spinach and has become a modern health-food staple.
Bourbon, fresh mint, and sugar syrup are the three key ingredients in this classic Southern cocktail.
Herb Peterson, a McDonald's franchisee, is credited with inventing this breakfast sandwich in 1971.

categories.title: EINSTEIN ON THE CASE
categories.theme: Facts about Albert Einstein's life and scientific legacy

clues.clue: This simple formula, H₂O, describes the molecular makeup of water — a substance Einstein famously used in thought experiments about light.
Einstein learned mathematics in the 1890s under this professor, whose name was later given to four-dimensional spacetime.
Einstein was awarded the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics for his explanation of this phenomenon, not for relativity.
The atomic mass of carbon — the backbone of organic chemistry — is this number.
Ozone, the molecule that shields Earth from ultraviolet radiation, has this molecular formula.

categories.title: WORDS & WORLDS
categories.theme: Classic and landmark works of literature and their characters or authors

clues.clue: Tigger makes his first appearance in this beloved children's book set in the Hundred Acre Wood.
The character Alonso Quijano is the real name of the title knight in this 17th-century Spanish novel.
In this Aldous Huxley dystopian novel, the words 'Mother' and 'Father' are considered obscene and are forbidden.
The character Tuor, a mortal man who weds an elf and fathers Eärendil, appears in this posthumous Tolkien mythology.
The sprawling fantasy 'Earthsea' series, featuring the wizard Ged, was written by this American author.

categories.title: HIDDEN CAPITALS

clues.clue: This northernmost borough of New York City shares its name with a county in Ireland.
Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam's largest metropolis, was known by this French colonial-era name before 1975.
This Balkan city on the Sava and Danube rivers serves as the capital of Serbia.
This East African capital is the seat of government for the world's newest nation, which gained independence in 2011.
This small kingdom, entirely surrounded by South Africa, is the only country in the world contained wholly within another country's borders.

finals.title: LITERARY FIRST LINES
finals.clue: The celebrated opening — 'In my younger and more vulnerable years my father gave me some advice that I've been turning over in my mind ever since' — begins this iconic American novel.

finals.title: MOTOR RACING HISTORY
finals.clue: At the 2016 24 Hours of Le Mans, this legendary German automaker claimed victory, continuing its storied endurance racing legacy.

finals.title: PHILOSOPHY OF FREEDOM
finals.clue: This French existentialist philosopher, author of 'Being and Nothingness,' famously declared that 'Man is condemned to be free,' suggesting freedom is an inescapable burden.

finals.title: SILVER SCREEN SHADOWS
finals.clue: In this 1950 Billy Wilder classic, she played faded silent-film star Norma Desmond, delivering the iconic line 'I am big — it's the pictures that got small.'

finals.title: REVOLUTIONARY INK
finals.clue: Though Karl Marx is the more famous name on the cover, this German philosopher and textile heir co-authored 'The Communist Manifesto' in 1848.
